description: ADAS Decision Service → Blockchain Module handshake payload specification

timestamp.description: Unix epoch timestamp (seconds) when the ADAS event was generated

event_type.description: The lane departure event classification issued by the ADAS Decision Service

driver.description: Full name of the driver at the time of the event

confidence.description: AI model confidence score for the lane departure detection [0.0–100.0]

notes: Payload MUST be a compact single-line JSON string (no whitespace between keys/values) for deterministic SHA-256 hashing.
The C++ DecisionService serialises the payload, hashes it with CryptoModule (OpenSSL SHA-256), and publishes both the JSON and the hash over ZMQ.
The Python Web3Bridge subscribes to both topics: ADAS_EVENT for local logging, ADAS_HASH for blockchain submission.
The Auditor.py script re-hashes each line in vehicle_log.txt and calls ForensicLogger.verifyHash(bytes32) for O(1) on-chain verification.
